data.HEADLINE: Summer Body Starts in the Kitchen
data.BODY: You can't out-train a bad diet. Focus on nutrient-dense whole foods, adequate protein, and proper hydration as temperatures rise. Your body composition is 80% nutrition.
data.STAT_LABEL: of body composition results come from nutrition
data.TIP_TEXT: Half your plate: colorful vegetables. Quarter: lean protein (chicken, fish, tofu). Quarter: complex carbs (sweet potato, brown rice). Add healthy fat from avocado or olive oil.

data.HEADLINE: Electrolytes: More Than Just Salt
data.BODY: When you sweat, you lose sodium, potassium, magnesium, and calcium. Plain water alone won't fully rehydrate you during intense summer training. Balance matters.
data.STAT_LABEL: of sweat lost per hour in hot weather exercise
data.TIP_TEXT: Mix 16 oz water + 1/8 tsp salt + juice of half a lemon + 1 tbsp honey. Cheaper and cleaner than store-bought sports drinks.

data.HEADLINE: Barcode Scanning: Instant Nutrition Data
data.SUBTITLE: Scan any packaged food and get full macro breakdown in under a second
data.FEATURE1_DESC: NourishAI reads UPC/EAN codes from any packaged food product instantly
data.FEATURE2_DESC: See full nutrition facts and adjust serving size to match what you actually ate
data.FEATURE3_DESC: One tap to add it to your daily log with accurate macros and calories

data.HEADLINE: Cutting vs. Bulking: Macro Differences
data.BODY: Cutting requires a 300-500 calorie deficit with high protein (1g/lb) to preserve muscle. Bulking needs a 200-300 surplus with adequate carbs to fuel training and growth.
data.STAT_LABEL: Protein per pound of bodyweight while cutting
data.TIP_TEXT: Cut for 8-12 weeks, then reverse diet for 4 weeks, then maintain or bulk. NourishAI adjusts your macro targets automatically when you update your goal.

data.HEADLINE: Healthy Grilling: Macro-Friendly BBQ
data.BODY: Grilling is one of the healthiest cooking methods when done right. Fat drips off, you don't need oil, and smoky flavor means less sauce needed. Perfect for high-protein summer eating.
data.STAT_LABEL: Added fat needed for grilled chicken breast
data.TIP_TEXT: Grill: chicken thighs (skin-off), salmon fillets, shrimp skewers, veggie kabobs. Skip: fatty sausages, sugar-loaded BBQ sauce, mayo-heavy coleslaw.

data.HEADLINE: Cook for Mom: A Healthy Brunch Menu
data.SUBTITLE: Show her you care with a nutritious and delicious Mother's Day spread
data.FEATURE1_DESC: Eggs, spinach, bell peppers, feta. 280 cal, 22g protein per slice
data.FEATURE2_DESC: Greek yogurt layered with mixed berries and granola. 310 cal, 24g protein
data.FEATURE3_DESC: Whole grain bread, smashed avo, poached egg, everything seasoning. 350 cal, 14g protein

data.HEADLINE: Creatine: The Most Researched Supplement
data.BODY: Creatine monohydrate is the most studied supplement in sports nutrition with proven benefits for strength, power, and muscle recovery. Safe, effective, and affordable.
data.STAT_LABEL: Daily maintenance dose of creatine monohydrate
data.TIP_TEXT: Take 5g daily with water — timing doesn't matter. No loading phase needed. Mix in your post-workout shake or morning coffee. Results appear after 2-4 weeks.

data.HEADLINE: Slow Down: The Power of Mindful Eating
data.BODY: It takes 20 minutes for your brain to register fullness. Eating slowly reduces total intake by 10-15% naturally. Put your fork down between bites, chew thoroughly, and enjoy every meal.
data.STAT_LABEL: For your brain to register satiety signals
data.TIP_TEXT: Set a timer for 20 minutes and try to make your meal last. No phone, no TV. Focus on textures and flavors. You'll eat less and enjoy more.

data.HEADLINE: HealthKit Integration: The Full Picture
data.SUBTITLE: NourishAI syncs with Apple Health to connect nutrition with your fitness data
data.FEATURE1_DESC: Calories burned from workouts automatically adjust your daily nutrition targets
data.FEATURE2_DESC: Scale readings from HealthKit feed directly into your progress charts
data.FEATURE3_DESC: See how your evening meals affect sleep quality over time

data.HEADLINE: Sunday Meal Prep: The 90-Minute System
data.BODY: Batch cooking saves time, money, and willpower. Prep 4-5 days of lunches and snacks in under 90 minutes. The key: choose recipes that share ingredients and reheat well.
data.TIP_TEXT: Grill 2 lbs chicken breast, cook 3 cups rice, roast a sheet pan of mixed veggies, boil a dozen eggs, portion out Greek yogurt + berries in 5 containers.

data.HEADLINE: Collegiate Athlete: Fueling for Performance
data.BEFORE_STAT1: Undereating on training days by 800+ cal
data.BEFORE_STAT3: Frequent muscle cramps and slow recovery
data.AFTER_STAT1: Properly fueling with 3,200 cal on game days
data.AFTER_STAT3: Recovery time cut in half, zero cramps

data.HEADLINE: Protein Sources Ranked by Quality
data.BODY: Not all proteins are equal. Complete proteins contain all 9 essential amino acids. Leucine content drives muscle protein synthesis. Animal sources are complete; plant sources often need combining.
data.STAT_LABEL: Leucine threshold to trigger muscle growth
data.TIP_TEXT: 1) Eggs (the gold standard, 6g each). 2) Chicken breast (31g per 4oz). 3) Greek yogurt (20g per cup). 4) Wild salmon (25g per 4oz + omega-3s). 5) Lentils (18g per cup, plant-based).

data.FOOD_NAME: Poke Bowl with Tuna, Edamame & Brown Rice

data.HEADLINE: Macro-Friendly Cookout Guide
data.SUBTITLE: Enjoy the holiday without derailing your nutrition goals
data.FEATURE1_DESC: Turkey burgers (93% lean), grilled chicken, or shrimp kabobs over fatty hot dogs
data.FEATURE2_DESC: Grilled corn on the cob, watermelon, or black bean salad instead of mayo-laden sides
data.FEATURE3_DESC: Log your cookout meal with NourishAI and adjust dinner to stay on track for the day

data.HEADLINE: Anti-Inflammatory Foods for Faster Recovery
data.BODY: Chronic inflammation from training without proper nutrition slows recovery and increases injury risk. Certain foods naturally reduce inflammation and support repair.
data.STAT_LABEL: Servings of anti-inflammatory foods per day
data.TIP_TEXT: Fatty fish (salmon, sardines), blueberries, turmeric with black pepper, tart cherry juice, leafy greens, walnuts, and extra virgin olive oil.

data.HEADLINE: Going Vegetarian Without Losing Gains
data.AFTER_STAT3: Strategic combining: legumes + grains every meal

data.HEADLINE: Your Gut Health Affects Everything
data.BODY: A healthy gut microbiome improves nutrient absorption, immune function, mood, and even body composition. Feed your good bacteria with diverse fiber sources and fermented foods.
data.STAT_LABEL: Different plant foods per week for optimal gut diversity
data.TIP_TEXT: Morning: probiotic yogurt or kefir. Lunch: add sauerkraut or kimchi. Dinner: include prebiotic foods (garlic, onions, asparagus). NourishAI tracks your plant food variety.
